turns.text: It is not -- it is not for all employees nor for a class. It is only --

turns.name: JUSTICE BLACK
turns.text: And even -- and even as to them, as I understand it, it is provided that if the Court – if the union developed and shows the Court how much they're spending for political purposes, they let them pay the rest?

turns.name: WHITEFORD S. BLAKENEY
turns.text: That is right, sir.

turns.name: TOM C. CLARK
turns.text: How much for the dues amount?

turns.name: WHITEFORD S. BLAKENEY
turns.text: The dues, sir, have varied. I do not actually know the amount now but I believe it's in the neighborhood of $5. It amounts to $8 million per year, their record shows in the case of one of this union.

turns.name: TOM C. CLARK
turns.text: [Inaudible]

turns.name: WHITEFORD S. BLAKENEY
turns.text: Months, sir.

turns.name: JUSTICE BRENNAN
turns.text: [Inaudible]

turns.name: WHITEFORD S. BLAKENEY
turns.text: Yes sir, that's what (Inaudible).

turns.name: JUSTICE WHITE
turns.text: [Inaudible]

turns.name: WHITEFORD S. BLAKENEY
turns.text: The injunction here, Your Honor, I'm not sure I understood you; the injunction here does not restrain anything about expenditure or whatever. The unions are totally free under this order to spend the money they get for any purpose they may see fit to as long as they don't run afoul of some law like Federal Corrupt Practice Act, not relevant here.

turns.name: JUSTICE BRENNAN
turns.text: [Inaudible]

turns.name: WHITEFORD S. BLAKENEY
turns.text: Your Honors eliminated the class aspect.

turns.name: JUSTICE BRENNAN
turns.text: Well -- no, that is not the sentence, the [Inaudible] to that opinion has to be read in the context [Inaudible] -- exactly like this with a few plaintiffs involved and where -- what you said that related to the remedies that those few plaintiffs in their individual interest would be entitled.

turns.name: WHITEFORD S. BLAKENEY
turns.text: True, Your Honor, that's one of the defects you find in the judgment, class action. Then you went on to enumerate the other defects about the injunction sweeping too broadly and (Inaudible) --

turns.name: JUSTICE BRENNAN
turns.text: Well, you dissent -- are you suggesting that the opinion [Inaudible] are you suggesting of the opinions are -- absolutely reject the class action, aspect of them, that both of the injunctions [Inaudible] class action rather than as individual plaintiffs?

turns.name: WHITEFORD S. BLAKENEY
turns.text: Oh, no, sir. I'm not saying that at all. I say that you first refer to the class action defect and you send it back, item one on that account. Then you referred to the aspect that it restrained all collections from the plaintiffs.

turns.name: JUSTICE BRENNAN
turns.text: That's right, [Inaudible]

turns.name: WHITEFORD S. BLAKENEY
turns.text: Yes, sir. And you send it back for that reason and I'm saying this judgment has none of those defects.

turns.name: JUSTICE BRENNAN
turns.text: Well, except for the injunction, [Inaudible]

turns.name: WHITEFORD S. BLAKENEY
turns.text: But the second part --

turns.name: JUSTICE BRENNAN
turns.text: [Inaudible] it does have the state court --

turns.name: WHITEFORD S. BLAKENEY
turns.text: Your Honor, the sec --

turns.name: JUSTICE BRENNAN
turns.text: [Inaudible] but nevertheless the practical --

turns.name: WHITEFORD S. BLAKENEY
turns.text: Your Honor, the second paragraph is as much a part of the judgment as the first paragraph. And the second paragraph says that if a union would tell us what parts, the collective bargaining, we must pay it and will pay it.

turns.name: JUSTICE BRENNAN
turns.text: I appreciate that argument but the fact is nonetheless [Inaudible]

turns.name: WHITEFORD S. BLAKENEY
turns.text: They have put up bond and have said to the union, “Now, tell us what you want for collective bargaining and we'll pay it.”

turns.name: JUSTICE BRENNAN
turns.text: [Inaudible]

turns.name: WHITEFORD S. BLAKENEY
turns.text: No, sir we're not paying dues but we're ready to pay.

turns.name: JUSTICE BLACK
turns.text: They're not getting --

turns.name: WHITEFORD S. BLAKENEY
turns.text: Collect --

turns.name: JUSTICE BLACK
turns.text: They are not getting any protection that they would get from having their money used for political purposes they're against either, are they?

turns.name: WHITEFORD S. BLAKENEY
turns.text: No, sir.

turns.name: JUSTICE BLACK
turns.text: The only way they could get it is the way the Court did it, isn't it?

turns.name: WHITEFORD S. BLAKENEY
turns.text: There is no other way. As a practical matter, there is no other way.
